creator: junchoon@dec.sakura.ne.jp
text:
Additional info.
My HW probe: See [1] at the bottom.

Tried bz@'s workaround introduced at Comment 181 (for me, `devctl detach xhci1` BEFORE detaching) worked fine for me. Thanks, bz@!

The TB3 controller seems to behave as follows:
 *On boot, standby and expose nothing.

 *If any device is attached to TB3 / USB type-C port, sniff it and decide
  which controller device (XHCI or TB3) to expose.

 *Hide controller device when attached external device is detached and standby.

 *If detached device is re-attached just after the detach, and it is USB one,
  silently re-route to other XHCI-only controller. Observed once for now, by 
  trying bz@'s workaround and re-attaching the same device (this time, uaudio),
  xhci1 didn't re-appear and uaudio appeared.
  If attached device was different one, xhci1 re-appeared.
  This behavior would be useful for not-enough-stable cable or connector.

Maybe the third behavior is breaking things for us.


Differences between before attaching and detaching the type-C hub.
Others appearing while the hub is attached are sanely dissappeard.
